entity.name: U.S. Congress (general)
entity.description: Catch-all for AI-related bills, resolutions, and floor activity in either chamber of the U.S. Congress not pinned to a specifically-tracked legislator. Backed by the congress.gov adapter scoping to AI-keyword-tagged bills regardless of sponsor; lets the system surface AI legislative activity from members outside the named-individuals roster.

activity.title: Investigations and Oversight Subcommittee Hearing - Powering America's AI Future: Assessing Policy Options to Increase Data Center Infrastructure
activity.raw_text: 119th Congress Hearing Artificial Intelligence Oversight · Chairman Brian Babin · Investigations and Oversight Subcommittee Chairman Rich McCormick · Ms. Paige Lambermont, Research Fellow, Competitive Enterprise Institute

activity.title: Hearing Recap: Building an AI-Ready America: Adopting AI at Work
activity.raw_text: Today, the Subcommittee on Health, Employment, Labor, and Pensions held a hearing to help ensure American workers and job creators are ready to compete and succeed in an economy driven by artificial intelligence (AI). Subcommittee Chairman Rick Allen (R-GA) started the hearing by discussing how clear expectations and responsible use of AI not only protect workers' rights but also strengthen workplace morale and performance.

_verify_phrases: ready to compete and succeed in an economy driven by artificial intelligence
